# UC-10.16.6 ┬Ę Firewall Rule Hit Rate Trending

## Description

Top rules by hit count over 30 days expose noisy policies, overly broad permits, and candidates for segmentation review. Flat or rising hit rates on ŌĆ£catch-allŌĆØ rules often precede policy hygiene projects.

## Value

Top rules by hit count over 30 days expose noisy policies, overly broad permits, and candidates for segmentation review. Flat or rising hit rates on ŌĆ£catch-allŌĆØ rules often precede policy hygiene projects.

## Implementation

If `rule` is missing, derive from `policy_id` or `rule_uid` via vendor TA. Exclude health-check and internal monitoring sources with a subnet lookup. Use accelerated DM or summary `sourcetype=stash` for very high volume. Pair with change tickets when hit rates jump after policy pushes.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_paloalto`, `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, Cisco Secure Firewall Add-on, `Splunk_TA_juniper` (SRX), Splunk Common Information Model.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `Network_Traffic` data model (firewall allow/deny), `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic` or equivalent.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
If `rule` is missing, derive from `policy_id` or `rule_uid` via vendor TA. Exclude health-check and internal monitoring sources with a subnet lookup. Use accelerated DM or summary `sourcetype=stash` for very high volume. Pair with change tickets when hit rates jump after policy pushes.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count FROM datamodel=Network_Traffic WHERE nodename=All_Traffic All_Traffic.action IN ("allowed","accept") earliest=-30d
    BY _time span=1d All_Traffic.rule
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| timechart span=1d sum(count) as hits by rule useother=f limit=10
```

Understanding this SPL

**Firewall Rule Hit Rate Trending** ŌĆö Top rules by hit count over 30 days expose noisy policies, overly broad permits, and candidates for segmentation review. Flat or rising hit rates on ŌĆ£catch-allŌĆØ rules often precede policy hygiene projects.

Documented **Data sources**: `Network_Traffic` data model (firewall allow/deny), `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic` or equivalent. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_paloalto`, `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, Cisco Secure Firewall Add-on, `Splunk_TA_juniper` (SRX), Splunk Common Information Model. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic` ŌĆö enable acceleration for that model.
ŌĆó Invokes macro `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` ŌĆö in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
ŌĆó `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by rule useother=f limit=10** ŌĆö ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Compare a policy **change ticket**ŌĆÖs time to the start of a hit-rate jump so you can tell a rule push from a real attack window.



Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (top rules by hits), line chart (daily hits for top five rules), treemap (rule share of total hits).


## SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count FROM datamodel=Network_Traffic WHERE nodename=All_Traffic All_Traffic.action IN ("allowed","accept") earliest=-30d
    BY _time span=1d All_Traffic.rule
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| timechart span=1d sum(count) as hits by rule useother=f limit=10
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (top rules by hits), line chart (daily hits for top five rules), treemap (rule share of total hits).
